data.content:
There are **no .NET runtime frames** in this tombstone. The backtrace contains only:

- `libc.so` — Android C library
- `libutils.so` — Android system utilities
- `libnativeapp.so` — the app's own JNI native library
- `libart.so` — Android ART runtime

This is a pure native Android/ART crash (SIGSEGV in `strlen`, called from a JNI function `Java_com_example_NativeLib_processData`). There are no .NET libraries (e.g., `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonosgen-2.0.so`) present, so there's nothing to symbolicate on the .NET side.
data.reasoningText:
This tombstone file is from a native Android app (`com.example.nativeapp`). Looking at the backtrace, I can see:

- Frame #00: `libc.so` - standard C library (`strlen`)
- Frame #01: `libutils.so` - Android system utility
- Frame #02: `libnativeapp.so` - the app's native library (JNI function)
- Frame #03-#05: `libart.so` - Android Runtime (ART)

There are no .NET runtime frames in this tombstone at all. There's no `libcoreclr.so`, `libmonosgen-2.0.so`, or any other .NET-related library. This is a pure native Android/ART crash — no .NET runtime is involved.
